checks.finding: 'lite track' is referenced in Non-goals and AC-009 but never defined; 'lite-track behavior for critical tasks' has no defined content, so implementers could reach opposite conclusions for the safety control REQ-004 guarantees.

Non-goals excludes cross-model on the lite track while REQ-004 mandates cross-model for Risk:critical/Security-Sensitive:true unconditionally and AC-008 requires the flow to stop absent cross-model/waiver; the spec never states whether a critical task on the lite track is exempt (contradicting REQ-004) or blocked from lite (leaving the non-goal unclear). Direct Goal/Non-goal conflict on a safety-relevant workflow boundary.
Every Edge Cases scenario maps to a specific acceptance test (ASCII->AC-015, cd/pushd->AC-003/005, prefix collision and absent dir->AC-006, no-PR->AC-014, read-target and malformed->AC-012/013).
All four Assumptions are stable premises, implementation-verifiable facts, or wired into an AC forcing resolution (id-token->AC-010). Open Questions closed with recorded resolutions.
In-scope approval boundaries are testable: R-10 protected-path denial (AC-001/002/004) and Cross-Model-Waiver human-approval gate (AC-008/009); B1-B3 boundaries each backed by an AC/TEST pair.

AC-009 obligates documenting lite-track behavior for critical tasks but the requirements never state it, and the REQ-004/non-goal contradiction is unreconciled, so a downstream reviewer would invent the product rule for critical-task/lite-track interaction.
